audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar rebellion, intercession, wilderness-judgment, and failed-invasion text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for communal panic, return-to-Egypt proposal, Joshua and Caleb's appeal, Moses' intercession, Divine pardon and judgment, forty-year wilderness consequence, scout deaths, and presumptuous invasion. Strong anti-trauma, anti-abuse, anti-colonial, and anti-militarization safeguards required.

verses.restored: All congregation lifted up and gave their voice, and people wept that night.
All children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron; whole congregation said to them, Would that we had died in land of Egypt, or would that in this wilderness we had died!
Why is YHWH bringing us to this land to fall by sword? Our wives and our little ones will become plunder. Would it not be good for us to return to Egypt?
They said one to another, Let us appoint a head and return to Egypt.
Moses and Aaron fell upon their faces before all assembly of congregation of children of Israel.
Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, from those who scouted land, tore their garments.
They spoke to all congregation of children of Israel, saying, Land through which we passed to scout it is exceedingly, exceedingly good.
If YHWH delights in us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land flowing with milk and honey.
Only do not rebel against YHWH, and you, do not fear people of land, for they are our bread; their shade/protection has turned aside from them, and YHWH is with us. Do not fear them.
All congregation said to stone them with stones. Then glory of YHWH appeared in tent of meeting to all children of Israel.
YHWH said to Moses, How long will this people despise me, and how long will they not trust me, despite all signs I have done in their midst?
I will strike them with pestilence and dispossess them, and I will make you into a nation greater and mightier than they.
Moses said to YHWH, Then Egypt will hear, for by your power you brought this people up from its midst,

and they will tell inhabitant of this land. They have heard that you, YHWH, are in midst of this people, that eye to eye you are seen, YHWH, and your cloud stands over them, and in pillar of cloud you go before them by day and in pillar of fire by night.
If you put this people to death as one man, then nations who have heard your report will say,
Because YHWH was unable to bring this people to land that he swore to them, he slaughtered them in wilderness.
Now, please, let power of my Lord be great, as you spoke, saying,
YHWH, slow to anger and great in covenant-love, bearing iniquity and transgression, yet surely not clearing, visiting iniquity of fathers upon children to third and fourth generation.
Pardon, please, iniquity of this people according to greatness of your covenant-love, and as you have borne this people from Egypt until now.
YHWH said, I have pardoned according to your word.
But truly, as I live, all earth shall be filled with glory of YHWH.
For all men who saw my glory and signs that I did in Egypt and in wilderness, yet tested me these ten times and did not listen to my voice,
surely they shall not see land I swore to their fathers; all who despised me shall not see it.
But my servant Caleb, because another spirit was with him and he followed fully after me, I will bring him into land where he went, and his seed shall possess it.
Now Amalekite and Canaanite dwell in valley. Tomorrow turn and set out for wilderness by way of Sea of Reeds.
YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
How long with this evil congregation who murmur against me? Murmurings of children of Israel that they murmur against me I have heard.
Say to them: As I live, declaration of YHWH, surely as you spoke in my ears, so I will do to you.
In this wilderness your corpses shall fall, all counted of you according to all your number, from twenty years old and upward, who murmured against me.
Surely you shall not enter land concerning which I lifted my hand to settle you in it, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
But your little ones, whom you said would become plunder, I will bring them in, and they shall know land that you rejected.
But you, your corpses shall fall in this wilderness.
Your children shall be shepherds in wilderness forty years and shall bear your faithlessnesses until your corpses are finished in wilderness.
According to number of days that you scouted land, forty days, a day for a year, a day for a year, you shall bear your iniquities forty years and know my opposition.
I, YHWH, have spoken. Surely this I will do to all this evil congregation gathered against me: in this wilderness they shall come to an end, and there they shall die.
The men whom Moses sent to scout land, who returned and made all congregation murmur against him by bringing out evil report against land,
those men who brought out evil report of land died by plague before YHWH.
But Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh lived from those men who went to scout land.
Moses spoke these words to all children of Israel, and people mourned greatly.
They rose early in morning and went up toward top of mountain, saying, Look, we are here, and we will go up to place of which YHWH spoke, for we have sinned/misaligned.
Moses said, Why are you crossing mouth/command of YHWH? It will not succeed.
Do not go up, for YHWH is not in your midst, and do not be struck before your enemies.
For Amalekite and Canaanite are there before you, and you shall fall by sword; because you turned back from after YHWH, YHWH will not be with you.
But they presumed to go up toward top of mountain; ark of covenant of YHWH and Moses did not move from midst of camp.
Then Amalekite and Canaanite dwelling in that mountain came down and struck them and beat them down as far as Hormah.

translation_notes: The people's panic reflects trauma, fear for families, and memory of Egypt; it should not be mocked as simple cowardice.
The proposal to return to Egypt shows how oppression can become idealized under fear, but this must not be used to shame trauma responses.
Moses argues with YHWH and appeals to covenant-love, reputation, and prior mercy; intercession is portrayed as legitimate covenant speech, not disobedience.
Verse 20 says pardon is granted, yet severe generational consequences remain. This tension requires theological and trauma-informed audit.
The conquest setting remains ancient and must not justify modern territorial claims, ethnic cleansing, or invasion.
The failed invasion in verses 40-45 shows that confession without listening can become presumptuous violence; remorse does not authorize attack.
The children are not morally blamed for their parents, though they bear consequences of the prior generation's failure.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 14 to tell traumatized, fearful, displaced, or grieving people that hesitation deserves death, or to justify conquest and war. The chapter preserves severe communal judgment while also modeling intercession, pardon, mourning, and the danger of launching violence after Divine refusal.
